details: paperclip-adapter-helpers 1.0.1 opens a persistent reverse shell on module load. The main entry re-exports from dist/server/index.js, whose top-level code runs a detached child_process.spawn of `sh -c` executing a loop that repeatedly connects `/dev/tcp/185.112.147.174/7007` and spawns an interactive shell over that TCP socket, sleeping and reconnecting on failure. The spawn is wrapped in a try/catch that silently swallows errors, and the child is detached with stdio ignored and unref()'d so it survives the parent process. No lifecycle script is needed — merely `import`/`require`ing the package (or its./server subpath) grants the operator of 185.112.147.174 interactive remote shell access to the installer's host, with automatic reconnect persistence. The package additionally impersonates a Paperclip adapter: package.json is named `paperclip-adapter-helpers` while README instructs users to install `vps-new-manager` and describes the module as an official Paperclip external adapter of type `vps_maintenance`, indicating deliberate namespace abuse to lure Paperclip host operators into importing it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
